title: Tighten rebuild/resume logic and tx_state validation
summary: Strengthen rebuild prerequisites, schema validation, and next_action derivation to match 0.4.0 resume specs.
outputs: Rebuild requires tx_event_log presence per recovery algorithm
tx_state validation enforces schema_version and required fields
next_action derivation incorporates semantic_summary and user_intent
Resume always re-derives next_action from canonical state
Commit boundary metadata persisted in materialized state
acceptance_criteria: Rebuild fails or blocks when the transaction event log is missing.
tx_state_save rejects states missing schema_version, active_tx core fields, or integrity metadata.
next_action derivation uses semantic memory deterministically and aligns with resume_logic.md.
Resume logic re-derives next_action from canonical artifacts even when a prior session is selected.
Commit boundary metadata (branch/commit hash/diff summary) is persisted around tx.commit.start/done.

steps.description: Tighten tx_state validation to require schema_version and required active_tx fields.
Require tx_event_log presence for rebuild and handle missing log as blocked state.
Update next_action derivation to incorporate semantic_summary and user_intent guidance.
Persist commit boundary metadata in tx_state and ensure resume always re-derives next_action from canonical state.

notes: Keep deterministic behavior: same log yields same state and next_action.
Ensure changes align with resume_logic.md and semantic-memory-rules.md.
